text.title: Hybrid Search Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for hybrid retrieval config.

rule.statement: Default fusion is Reciprocal Rank Fusion with k=60. Switch to linear combination ONLY when RRF is measurably worse on a held-out set AND team can normalise + tune.

rationale.source: Cormack et al. 2009 RRF paper; LlamaIndex retrieval benchmarks

rule.rationale: RRF uses rank positions only, ignoring score magnitudes. Linear combination needs min-max or z-score normalisation; without it, BM25's unbounded scores dominate.

rule.statement: Prefer native hybrid endpoints (Qdrant `FusionQuery`, Weaviate `hybrid`, Pinecone sparse-dense, ES knn+BM25) over manual two-call fusion in application code.
rule.rationale: Manual fusion = two network calls + two failure modes + custom RRF impl. Native endpoints do it in one call with fewer corner cases.

rule.statement: When using linear fusion, alpha is computed per-query: quoted phrase → 0.2; numeric / product code → 0.3; ≤ 3-token query → 0.5; natural-language ≥ 4 tokens → 0.7.

rationale.source: Microsoft Bing hybrid; internal RAG benchmarks

rule.rationale: A fixed alpha is suboptimal across query distributions; a lightweight classifier adds negligible overhead but lifts precision by 5-10 pts.

rule.statement: When cross-encoder reranker is enabled, fetch top-100 from fusion and rerank to top-10. Never feed only top-10 to the reranker.
rule.rationale: Reranker can only re-order what it sees; small candidate pools cap its upside and waste its 50-100 ms cost.

rule.statement: Hybrid config ships only if precision@10 ≥ 0.85 on the held-out set AND p99 latency ≤ budget. Below floor, fall back to the simpler retriever rather than ship hybrid.
rule.rationale: Hybrid that fails its precision floor is added complexity for no value. Better to ship the simpler retriever and re-eval at the next corpus refresh.
